name: Google Chrome
tagline: The world's most popular browser, built for the web by the web's biggest player
highlights: Unmatched extension library via Chrome Web Store
Seamless Google account sync across devices
Best-in-class web standards and DevTools
bestFor: Google ecosystem users who want maximum compatibility and extension choice without any friction

name: Firefox
tagline: The independent, open-source browser that keeps the web from being a monopoly
highlights: Fully open source with strong community governance
Enhanced Tracking Protection built-in
Deep customization via userChrome.css and extensions
bestFor: Users who value an open, independent web and want solid privacy without giving up usability

name: Safari
tagline: Apple's browser, engineered to get the most out of every MacBook charge
highlights: Best battery life of any macOS browser — by a wide margin
Tight iCloud Keychain and Handoff integration
Intelligent Tracking Prevention with on-device ML
bestFor: Apple device owners who want the fastest, most battery-efficient experience with zero configuration

name: Microsoft Edge
tagline: A Chromium browser that actually thought about productivity
highlights: Built-in Copilot AI sidebar with deep Microsoft 365 integration
Vertical tabs, workspaces, and sleeping tabs built-in
PDF editor, immersive reader, and Collections included
bestFor: Microsoft 365 users and Windows power users who want AI tools and productivity features out of the box

name: Brave
tagline: The privacy browser that doesn't compromise on speed
highlights: Built-in ad and tracker blocking — no extension needed
Chromium-based with full Chrome extension support
Brave Search, Brave News, and optional Brave Rewards
bestFor: Privacy-conscious users who want Chrome's compatibility without Google's data collection

name: Vivaldi
tagline: The browser that bends to your will — every single setting
highlights: Unmatched UI customization: themes, layouts, toolbar placement
Built-in tab stacking, tiling, and two-level tab bars
Integrated mail client, calendar, feed reader, and notes
bestFor: Power users who want complete control over every pixel of their browser experience

name: Opera
tagline: The social, feature-packed browser with a built-in VPN
highlights: Free built-in VPN with unlimited bandwidth
Side panel with WhatsApp, Telegram, Instagram, and more
Opera Flow for seamless mobile-desktop sharing
bestFor: Social media enthusiasts who want messaging apps and VPN baked in without installing extra software

name: Opera GX
tagline: The gaming browser — RAM limiter, GX sounds, and all the aesthetics
highlights: GX Control to hard-limit CPU and RAM usage per browser
Twitch, Discord, and gaming news integrated in the sidebar
Customizable RGB color themes and GX sound effects
bestFor: Gamers who want their browser to look and feel like a gaming peripheral and not hog system resources mid-match

name: Arc Browser
tagline: A total rethink of what a browser can be — spaces, Boosts, and no tab bar
highlights: Spaces and Profiles to separate work, personal, and projects
Boosts to restyle or add custom JS to any website
Command Bar replaces the address bar with a smarter, faster workflow
bestFor: Design-minded creatives and knowledge workers who want a browser that feels genuinely different
discontinuedNote: Active development has ended — Arc may receive limited support going forward.

name: Zen Browser
tagline: Firefox, reimagined with a calm, beautiful, distraction-free interface
highlights: Sidebar-first UI with vertical tabs and workspace separation
Built on Firefox with full Gecko privacy protections
Actively developed open source project with a growing community
bestFor: Users who want Firefox's privacy and open-source values wrapped in a minimal, modern interface

name: Tor Browser
tagline: Maximum anonymity — your traffic bounces through three relays before reaching its destination
highlights: Routes all traffic through the Tor onion network by default
Anti-fingerprinting measures built into every aspect of the browser
Access to .onion sites and censorship-blocked content
bestFor: Journalists, activists, and anyone who needs true anonymity and is willing to trade speed for it

name: LibreWolf
tagline: Firefox with every telemetry dial turned to zero — hardened from day one
highlights: Ships with uBlock Origin pre-installed and strict filtering defaults
Zero Mozilla telemetry, crash reporting, or phoning home
Frequent security updates that closely track upstream Firefox
bestFor: Privacy purists who want a hardened Firefox without having to configure it themselves

name: DuckDuckGo Browser
tagline: Simple privacy for everyone — no technical knowledge required
highlights: Fire Button instantly wipes tabs, cookies, and browsing data
Duck Player provides a clean, tracker-free YouTube experience
Privacy Grade on every site so you always know what's being blocked
bestFor: Non-technical users who want real privacy protections with zero configuration and no learning curve

name: Orion
tagline: WebKit speed and efficiency — with Chrome and Firefox extensions, and zero telemetry
highlights: Runs Chrome and Firefox extensions natively on WebKit
Built by Kagi — zero telemetry, no ads, user-funded
Exceptional battery life and performance on Apple Silicon
bestFor: Mac users who want Safari's efficiency with Chrome's extension library and none of Apple's compromises

name: Floorp
tagline: A Firefox fork for those who want vertical tabs and deep UI control
highlights: Vertical tabs and sidebar workspaces built into the UI
Fully open source, developed by a Japanese student team
Deeply customizable toolbar and panel layout
bestFor: Firefox power users who want vertical tab management and richer UI controls without extensions

name: Mullvad Browser
tagline: Designed by the Tor Project to make every user look identical — the ultimate anti-fingerprint browser
highlights: Fingerprint resistance baked into every browser setting by design
Developed in collaboration with the Tor Project
No account needed, no sync, minimal attack surface
bestFor: Privacy-first users who want Tor-level fingerprint protection without routing through the Tor network

name: Thorium
tagline: A performance-optimized Chromium build — compiled for speed, not compromise
highlights: Recompiled with AVX2/SSE4 optimizations for measurable Speedometer gains
Chrome-compatible with most extensions and sites
Strips some Google telemetry compared to stock Chromium
bestFor: Speed enthusiasts on desktop who want to squeeze every point out of their hardware's benchmark potential
